channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Tunnel used to smuggle migrants, weapons located near El Paso
item.description: A tunnel that has been used for a year is used to smuggle migrants, weapons and cash across the U.S.-Mexico border.
item.dc:creator: Ali Bradley, NewsNation

item.title: Commerce Secretary Raimondo offers a parting shot at Trump and advice for Democrats' future
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — When Commerce Secretary Gina Raimondo looks at Donald Trump, she says she sees a president-elect who for all of his aggressive talk is afraid to let America compete with the rest of the world, responding instead with tariffs and curtailing immigration.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Former Oakland mayor, longtime partner indicted in alleged bribery scheme
item.description: OAKLAND (AP) — Former Oakland Mayor Sheng Thao, her longtime partner and two local businessmen were named in an indictment unsealed Friday alleging the four conspired to funnel city business to companies owned by the men in exchange for money and benefits to Thao and her partner.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Will spring arrive early? What NOAA says
item.description: A La Niña winter just started, but it isn't expected to last long. National forecasters are already looking ahead to the spring season. A new long-range forecast released Thursday shows broad weather predictions for February, March and April nationwide.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, KTLA 5

item.title: Judges hand down what could be the last punishments for the Jan. 6 riot before pardons from Trump
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The federal judge who oversaw Donald Trump's 2020 election interference case denounced efforts to rewrite the history of the U.S. Capitol attack Friday as she handed down what she acknowledged may be one of the final punishments for the Jan.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Sealy Mattress company is sued after Georgia man was found dead inside a locked trailer
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — A Georgia man suffering a mental health crisis wandered into a Sealy Mattress facility, and his body was found locked inside the back of a truck there about a week later, his family says in a wrongful death lawsuit filed this week.
item.dc:creator: JEFF MARTIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Will spring come early? NOAA releases weather predictions for every state
item.description: A La Niña winter just started, but it isn't expected to last long. National forecasters are already looking ahead to the spring season.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: How to save your TikTok videos, data ahead of looming ban
item.description: Barring a last-minute turnaround, the popular social media app will be unavailable in all 50 states Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Will spring have showers? NOAA releases weather predictions for California
item.description: A La Niña winter just started, but it isn't expected to last long. National forecasters are already looking ahead to the spring season.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, Fox 5

item.title: Turnstile 'spikes' installed in NYC's crime-ridden subways as Hochul beefs up police presence
item.description: New York Gov. Kathy Hochul has announced a new $77 million plan to clamp down on subway crime, while the MTA has also begun installing spikes on some of its turnstiles to stop fare evaders.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump wants one of the agents who covered him after he was shot to lead the Secret Service
item.description: FORT LAUDERDALE, Fla. (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump will choose to lead the U.S. Secret Service one of the agents who covered him after a gunmen opened fire at the candidate during a campaign rally in Pennsylvania. Donald Trump Jr.
item.dc:creator: ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mexican influencers reportedly killed for ties to Sinaloa cartel
item.description: Bloodshed between members of the Sinaloa cartel may be common, but the death of influencers is a new development.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Senate advances Laken Riley Act, teeing up final vote
item.description: The Senate voted Friday to advance the Laken Riley Act, putting Republicans a step closer to sending the first bill of the 119th Congress to President-elect Trump for his signature next week and giving him an early win on one of his key issues.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Bruce Willis shakes hands with first responders in rare public appearance
item.description: Amid the devastating wildfires in Southern California, Bruce Willis took time to thank first responders.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, WGN 9

item.title: Supreme Court gives green light to law that could ban TikTok
item.description: WASHINGTON — The Supreme Court on Friday delivered a blow to TikTok by upholding a law that could potentially lead to the video-sharing social media platform being banned in the United States.
item.dc:creator: Lawrence Hurley and Kat Tenbarge, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Supreme Court upholds TikTok ban
item.description: The Supreme Court on Friday unanimously upheld a law requiring TikTok’s China-based parent company to divest from the app, teeing up a ban set to take effect Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, WGN 9

item.title: Mel Gibson ‘just as surprised’ as anyone to be named Trump ‘special envoy’ to Hollywood
item.description: Actor and film producer Mel Gibson said he was stunned to be appointed a special envoy to Hollywood under the upcoming Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KTLA 5

item.title: Capital One technical issue delays payments for users: What to know
item.description: Some Capital One users were still waiting to receive deposits and payments on Friday morning after the company experienced a processing disruption.
item.dc:creator: Dominique Jack, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Biden sets record by commuting sentences of nearly 2,500 people convicted on nonviolent drug charges
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden announced Friday that he was commuting the sentences of almost 2,500 people convicted of nonviolent drug offenses, using his final days in office on a flurry of clemency actions meant to nullify prison terms he deemed too harsh.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: WATCH: Fire at one of the world's largest battery plants forces evacuations in California
item.description: Hundreds of people were ordered to evacuate and part of Highway 1 in Northern California was closed after a major fire erupted at one of the world's largest battery storage plants.
item.dc:creator: KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Tony Award-winning British actor Joan Plowright, widow of Laurence Olivier, dies at 95
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Award-winning British actor Joan Plowright, who with her late husband Laurence Olivier did much to revitalize the U.K.'s theatrical scene in the decades after World War II, has died. She was 95.
item.dc:creator: MARK KENNEDY and PAN PYLAS, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Biden administration picks 15 more drugs for Medicare negotiation
item.description: The Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services (CMS) has selected 15 more drugs for Medicare price negotiation, announcing the selection two weeks ahead of schedule.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: In Guatemala and Minnesota, holy feast brings migrant families hope and pride amid crackdown fears
item.description: WORTHINGTON, Minn., (AP) — With their sparkling embroideries and colorful sashes flapping in the frigid wind, hundreds of Guatemalan faithful flocked to the Catholic church of this heartland farming town to celebrate their biggest festival yet in honor of the Black Christ of Esquipulas.
item.dc:creator: GIOVANNA DELL'ORTO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Biden commutes the sentences of nearly 2,500 non-violent drug offenders
item.description: Biden said he is proud of his record on clemency and will “continue to review additional commutations and pardons” before he leaves office on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, WGN 9

item.title: FDA will allow Zyn nicotine pouches to stay on the market
item.description: Zyn products will be allowed to stay on the market after federal health officials said Thursday that the popular nicotine pouches can help adult smokers cut back or switch completely.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Biden won't enforce TikTok ban, official says, leaving fate of app to Trump
item.description: President Joe Biden won't enforce a ban on the social media app TikTok that is set to take effect a day before he leaves office on Monday, a U.S. official said Thursday, leaving its fate in the hands of President-elect Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: SpaceX loses spacecraft after catching rocket booster at the launch pad in latest Starship test
item.description: SpaceX launched its Starship rocket on its latest test flight Thursday, but the spacecraft was destroyed following a thrilling booster catch back at the pad. Elon Musk’s company said Starship broke apart — what it called a “rapid unscheduled disassembly.
item.dc:creator: MARCIA DUNN, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Netanyahu says deal to release hostages held in Gaza has been reached after last minute snags
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said Friday a deal to return hostages held in the Gaza Strip has been reached, after his office said earlier there were last minute snags in finalizing a ceasefire that would pause 15 months of war.
item.dc:creator: TIA GOLDENBERG, WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Driver in Texas migrant smuggling run that led to the deaths of 53 people pleads guilty
item.description: A Texas truck driver charged in the deaths of 53 migrants who rode in a sweltering tractor-trailer with no air conditioning pleaded guilty Thursday over the 2022 tragedy that became the nation's deadliest smuggling attempt across the U.S.-Mexico border. Homero Zamorano Jr.
item.dc:creator: PAUL WEBER and LISA BAUMANN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: O'Reilly: Trump's past mob dealings prepared him to handle Putin
item.description: Bill O'Reilly thinks Trump's willingness to talk with anyone, even the mob, will serve him well when dealing with Putin.
item.dc:creator: Leland Vittert, NewsNation

item.title: Southern California native paid off mortgage day before Eaton Fire consumed home
item.description: While those displaced by the Eaton and Palisades fires are still reeling from the loss of their homes and, in some cases, loved ones, many of them say they’re being victimized yet again by price-gouging landlords.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Top music acts announced for FireAid, a concert to support L.A. wildfire victims
item.description: The biggest names in pop, rock and country are coming together to raise funds for victims of the Palisades and Eaton wildfires and assist with the recovery.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Armed LA residents defy evacuation orders to defend homes from wildfire looters
item.description: “I do have firearms and I’ve been calling my friends to make sure I know how to legally exist with them,” homeowner EveAnna Manley said.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Chow, NewsNation

item.title: Official portraits of Donald Trump, JD Vance are released
item.description: Not waiting until after his inauguration, on Thursday President-elect Trump gave the country a look at what he says are the official portraits of himself and Vice President JD Vance.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Drone collides with paraglider, forcing emergency landing
item.description: A paraglider who took off from Torrey Pines State Reserve in California was forced to make an emergency landing when a drone became entwined in his parachute.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, WFLA, NBC

item.title: For LA water issues, misinformation spreads nearly as fast as the wildfires
item.description: A billionaire couple was accused of withholding water that could help stop Los Angeles' massive wildfires. Democratic leadership was blamed for fire hydrants running dry and for an empty reservoir. Firefighters were criticized for allegedly using “women's handbags" to fight the fires.
item.dc:creator: MELISSA GOLDIN and BRITTANY PETERSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Palisades Fire firefighters taking part in first-of-its-kind cancer study
item.description: The study comes after the International Agency for Research on Cancer officially classified firefighting as a carcinogenic profession.
item.dc:creator: Juliette Smith, KRON4
